target.name: Flock Safety
target.founder: Garrett Langley, Matt Feury, Paige Todd

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $7.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.4B - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Skydio
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series E), post-money $2.2B

data.narrative:
Flock Safety, a Series E defense and security company founded by Garrett Langley, Matt Feury, and Paige Todd, carries a current post-money valuation of $7.5B following $950M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public entities and 8 private companies—within the Defense & Security sector, including adjacent-stage benchmarks like Saronic ($9.2B Series D), Chaos Industries ($4.5B Series D), Skydio ($2.2B Series E), and Epirus ($1.4B Series D).

Two triangulation methods apply to this valuation. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $7.5B last post-money round and applying a typical 1.4x Series E step-up multiplier, implies $10.5B. Peer-median valuation across the 4-company comparable set yields $3.4B, with a range spanning $1.4B to $9.2B. The methodological divergence reflects meaningful dispersion in how defense-tech companies are valued at this stage.

Synthesizing these approaches produces a triangulated range of $2.8B (low), $6.9B (median), and $12.1B (high). The current $7.5B valuation sits within this range but closer to the upper bound, supported primarily by stage-progression mechanics. Key uncertainties include the applicability of stage multiples in an illiquid, specialized sector and whether peer comparables—spanning nascent Series D to Series E rounds—adequately reflect Flock Safety's market position and operational maturity.
